title: viburnum erosum f. xanthocarpum
intro: Viburnum erosum f. xanthocarpum, also known as the Maple-leaved Arrowwood, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its maple-like leaves and yellow berries. This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Viburnum erosum f. xanthocarpum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
